steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The previous sentence describes tropical forests as humid with closely spaced trees. The blank introduces a sentence describing savannas as drier with trees spaced further apart. The transition must signal a direct comparison between two different environments.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘For instance,’: signals that what follows is an example of the preceding idea. Savannas are not an example of tropical forests; they are being contrasted with them. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘In comparison,’: directly signals that the description of savannas is being measured against the description of tropical forests in the previous sentence, which is exactly the relationship between the two clauses. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘Firstly,’: signals the first item in a numbered series. The passage is not listing a sequence of points; it is contrasting two environments. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘In conclusion,’: signals a closing summary. No conclusion is being drawn; the sentence introduces new descriptive information about savannas. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
